content.content:
- content: "Meeting notes\nWe discussed the new product features and the marketing\
    \ strategy in detail. The meeting was held in the main\nconference room and was\
    \ attended by the heads of the Development, Marketing, and Product teams. The\
    \ primary\nfocus was on enhancing the user experience and incorporating feedback\
    \ received from the initial market tests.\n\nAction items:\n  - Follow up with\
    \ the marketing team to finalize the go-to-market strategy. This includes setting\
    \ a timeline for the\n    campaign launch and identifying the key marketing channels.\n\
    \n  - Schedule a meeting with the development team to discuss the technical feasibility\
    \ of the proposed features.\n    It is crucial to assess the development timeline\
    \ and resource allocation.\n\n  - Update the product roadmap to reflect the new\
    \ features and timelines discussed. This updated roadmap\n    will be shared with\
    \ all stakeholders for transparency and to ensure alignment.\n\n  - Prepare a\
    \ competitive analysis report by analyzing the current market trends and competitor\
    \ products.\n    This will help in positioning our product effectively.\n\n  -\
    \ Organize a focus group session with select users to gather direct feedback on\
    \ the prototype.\n    This feedback will be invaluable in making the final adjustments\
    \ before the launch.\n\n  - Develop a contingency plan to address potential risks\
    \ identified during the meeting. This\n    includes supply chain disruptions,\
    \ technology challenges, and market entry barriers.\n\nThe next steps involve\
    \ each team working on their assigned tasks and reconvening in two weeks\nfor\
    \ a progress update. The goal is to launch the product by the end of the quarter,\
    \ and every\neffort will be made to adhere to this timeline.\n"

content.content:
I found the meeting notes (2024-05-08_product-meeting.docx, owner: alex.martin@bluesparrowtech.com). The action items in the notes are:

- Follow up with the marketing team to finalize the go-to-market strategy (timeline, channels).  
- Schedule a meeting with the development team to discuss technical feasibility, timeline, and resource allocation.  
- Update the product roadmap to reflect new features and timelines and share with stakeholders.  
- Prepare a competitive analysis report (market trends, competitor products).  
- Organize a focus group with select users to gather prototype feedback.  
- Develop a contingency plan to address risks (supply chain, tech, market entry).

Before I email “everyone mentioned,” I need one clarification: the notes refer to teams/roles (Marketing, Development, Product, stakeholders) but do not list individual email addresses except the document owner and people it’s shared with. How would you like me to proceed? Options:

1) I can send one tailored email to each team/role above (Marketing, Development, Product, Research/UX/Ops) using recipient addresses you provide (preferred if you have team aliases or specific leads). I will use the drafts below unless you edit them.

2) I can send the emails to the document owner (alex.martin@bluesparrowtech.com) and the people it’s already shared with (david.lee@bluesparrowtech.com and you). This would notify the owner and the known recipients and ask them to forward or assign tasks.

3) I can search your contacts for likely people (e.g., “Head of Marketing”, “Head of Development”, “Product Lead”) and propose a recipient list for your approval, then send.

Draft email templates (I can customize before sending):

- To Marketing team / Marketing lead
  Subject: Action — Finalize go-to-market strategy (from 2024-05-08 product meeting)
  Body: Summary of required actions: finalize timeline, identify key channels, prepare launch campaign plan. Deliverable: final go-to-market plan. Due: suggest a due date (please confirm). Please confirm who will own this and next steps.

- To Development team / Development lead
  Subject: Action — Schedule feasibility meeting (from 2024-05-08 product meeting)
  Body: Request a meeting to review proposed features, assess technical feasibility, timeline, and resource needs. Please propose 2–3 time slots in the next week and identify expected attendees.

- To Product owner / Roadmap owner
  Subject: Action — Update product roadmap (from 2024-05-08 product meeting)
  Body: Please update the roadmap with new features and timelines and share with stakeholders. Include version and change log. Suggested deadline: [please confirm].

- To Research/PM for competitive analysis
  Subject: Action — Prepare competitive analysis (from 2024-05-08 product meeting)
  Body: Please analyze current market trends and competitor products and submit a short report with recommended positioning. Suggested scope and deadline: [please confirm].

- To UX/Research for focus group
  Subject: Action — Organize focus group (from 2024-05-08 product meeting)
  Body: Please organize a focus group with select users, coordinate logistics, and gather feedback on the prototype. Request target participant profile and proposed dates.

- To Ops/Risk / All stakeholders re: contingency plan
  Subject: Action — Develop contingency plan (from 2024-05-08 product meeting)
  Body: Please draft a contingency plan addressing supply chain, technology, and market entry risks. Identify mitigation steps and owners.

Tell me:
- Which option do you want (1, 2, or 3)?  
- If option 1 or 2: provide the exact email addresses to send to (or confirm I should use alex.martin@bluesparrowtech.com and david.lee@bluesparrowtech.com).  
- Any edits to the draft subjects/bodies or deadlines you want included.

Once you confirm recipients and any edits, I’ll send the emails.
